rulings.Case Title: NATIONAL CREDIT ACCEPTANCE, INC A CALIFORNIAN CORP VS. MAHENDRA JHINGAN et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Granting An Earnings Withholdings On The Spouse On The Spouse Of A Judgment Debtor And Request For Judicial Notice
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, November 26, 2012, at 9:00 am, line 12, Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Granting An Earnings Withholdings On The Spouse Of A Judgment Debtor And Request For Judicial Notice Pro Tem Judge James Watson, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Grant the unopposed motion, subject to proof of notice to defendant. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to jamesw@skwsf.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/JPT

rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, November 26, 2012, Line 2, DEFENDANT ANTHONY AMERICO SIPOS AS AN INDIVIDUAL, AND DBA CUSTOM DOOR COMPANY'S AMENDED MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND. Off calendar. Duplicate entry. = (302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: CREDITORS TRADE ASSOCIATION INC DBA GREAT WESTERN COLLECTION BUREAU VS. ANTONIO CALVARIO AKA INDIVIDUALLY DBA CUSTOM
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, November 26, 2012, Line 2, DEFENDANT ANTHONY AMERICO SIPOS AS AN INDIVIDUAL, AND DBA CUSTOM DOOR COMPANY'S MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND. The motion to vacate the judgment entered on August 25, 2009, is granted. The judgment is improper as to Mr. Antonio Sipos. The Court denies the request for sanctions. Mr. Sipos relies on CCP 128.5, but that provision only applies to actions that were commenced prior to December 31, 1994. See CCP 128.5 (b)(1). Plaintiff may apply for entry of a new judgment. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/EHG)

To Hoem's Request For Production Of Documents Set One Numbers 3-6, 10, 11, 14-16 Pro Tem Judge James Watson, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. rulings.Case Title: WAUKEEN Q. MCCOY VS. KEN PAGE et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion Andm Otion For Determination Of Good Faith Settlement By Deft Kenneth Page; Request For Judicial Notice
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, November 26, 2012, Line 15, DEFENDANT KENNETH PAGE'S Motion For Determination Of Good Faith Settlement By Defendant Kenneth Page; Request For Judicial Notice. The motion is denied without prejudice. Defendant Page fails to present substantial evidence showing that the settlement was in the ballpark. See Tech-Bilt, Inc. v. Woodward-Clyde & Assoc., (1985) 38 Cal.3d 488. Defendant needs to present evidence showing a rough approximation of plaintiff's recovery and the defendants' proportionate liability. See Mattco Forge, Inc. v. Arthur Young & Co. (1995) 38 Cal.App.4th 1337, 1350, n.6. Defendant has not presented that evidence and did not comply with the Court's November 2 order to provide that supplemental evidence by November 16. The Court rejects Chicago Title's other arguments. The stay is lifted for the limited purpose of hearing this motion and Chicago Title is a joint tortfeasor. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: LLOYD O'NEAL VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, November 26, 2012, Line 13, DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. The motion for summary judgment is denied. The motion for summary adjudication is denied as to the first and fourth causes of action (first, second, sixth and seventh issues). Mr. O'Neal has presented triable issues of fact as to the expert opinions of Dr. Bubanja and Dr. Heinz regarding disability discrimination. Summary adjudication is granted as to the second and third causes of action (third through fifth issues). Mr. O'Neal cannot show Defendant's failure to accommodate or engage in the interactive process, Mr. O'Neal admits he neither sought nor requested accommodation. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and in compliance with Code of Civil Procedure 437c(g) and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/EHG)

rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, November 26, 2012, Line 11, DEFENDANT PROTRANSPORT-1, LLC'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. The demurrer is sustained with leave to amend. Assuming without deciding that Code of Civil Procedure section 338(a)'s one-year statute of limitations applies, Plaintiff seeks and is granted leave to amend to add additional plaintiffs whose employment terminated within the last year. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: JOSEPH LEE et al VS. NARANJIBHAI R PATEL et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Hearing On Motion And Motion To Compel Responses To Discovery From Pltf'S And Request For Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, November 26, 2012, at 9:00 am, line 10, DEFENDANTS NARANJIBHAI PATEL, INDIRABEN PATEL'S Notice Of Hearing On Motion And Motion To Compel Responses To Discovery From Plaintiff's And Request For Monetary Sanctions Pro Tem Judge James Watson, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Grant the motion. No sanctions. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to jamesw@skwsf.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/JPT

rulings.Case Title: DINTCHO VS. O'BRIEN
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn To Tax Costs; Memo Of P & A
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, November 26, 2012, Line 12, DEFENDANT MILTON O'BRIEN'S Motion To Tax Costs. Continued to December 6, 2012 per the Amended Notice filed on November 6, 2012. = (302/EHG)
